records.ety: [L. dysenteria, Gr. <?/; dys- ill, bad + <?/, pl. <?/, intestines, fr. 'ento`s within, fr. <?/ in, akin to E. in: cf. F. dysenterie. See Dys, and In.]

defs: A disease attended with inflammation and ulceration of the colon and rectum, and characterized by griping pains, constant desire to evacuate the bowels, and the discharge of mucus and blood.
notes: ☞ When acute, dysentery is usually accompanied with high fevers. It occurs epidemically, and is believed to be communicable through the medium of the alvine discharges.
